record.field: Neonatal (0–2 weeks)
Mother's milk only (colostrum is vital in the first hours). If orphaned, feed a puppy milk replacer every 2–3 hours, day and night — never cow's milk.
Keep warm (puppies cannot regulate temperature). Weigh daily — steady gain is the key health sign. No vaccines yet.
Transitional (2–4 weeks)
Still nursing. Near the end of this stage, introduce a soft gruel (puppy food soaked in warm water/milk replacer).
Eyes and ears open; first wobbly steps. First deworming around 2 weeks, then every 2 weeks. Gentle handling begins.
Puppy / Socialization (4–12 weeks)
Wean fully onto quality puppy food by ~6–8 weeks. Feed 3–4 small meals a day.
Critical socialization window — gentle exposure to people, sounds and handling. Core vaccination series starts ~6–8 weeks; continue deworming. Puppies must NOT be sold before 8 weeks of age.
Juvenile (3–6 months)
Quality puppy food, 3 meals a day reducing to 2. Match the formula to expected adult size (large-breed puppy food for big breeds).
Complete the vaccination series; keep up parasite prevention. Microchip by 12 weeks of age. Begin basic training and lead work.
Adolescent (6–12 months)
Two meals a day. Switch to adult food depends on size: small breeds ~9–12 months, large/giant breeds ~12–24 months.
Females may have their first heat (small breeds ~6 months, large breeds ~12–18 months) — monitor and plan. Discuss desexing timing with your vet. Continue training and exercise.
Adult (1–7 years)
Adult maintenance food, usually 2 meals a day. Watch body condition closely — obesity is the most common avoidable health problem.
Annual vet check, vaccination boosters, dental care, year-round parasite prevention. This is the prime breeding age for healthy, screened dogs.
Senior (7+ years)
Senior formula with adjusted calories and joint support; smaller, more frequent meals if needed. Large breeds reach senior earlier than small breeds.
Twice-yearly vet checks. Watch for arthritis, dental disease, weight change, and organ-related issues. Keep gentle daily exercise.
